rows.item: 学習意欲・授業参加度

summary: This seminar will focus on pronunciation and pronunciation -related language issues. Although all important elements of pronunciation will be addressed, the seminar will focus on prosody, or suprasegmentals, an important part of phonetics and pronunciation for language learners. Primary emphasis on suprasegmentals keeps the focus on the communicative aspect of language learning. Pronunciation -related language issues to be examined will be 1) the variety of English native-speaker (NS) accents 2) divergent varieties of English within NS contexts such as African American Vernacular English and 3) Non-native speaker (NNS) English language use, specifically focusing on issues related to English as a lingua franca.
aims:
Acquire basic skills for conducting professional research.
To improve students' listening comprehension and oral communication skills by focusing on the interplay between pronunciation, World Englishes, and suprasegmental features in real-world communication scenarios.
goals: To explore the cultural implications of pronunciation variations and World Englishes.
To promote sensitivity to diverse linguistic practices and enhance cross-cultural communication skills.

plan.text: Outline of suprasegmental aspects of pronunciation and familiarization of technical terms that will be featured during the semester’s instruction.
Creating an awareness of suprasegmentals; questionnaire on pronunciation to facilitate discussion.
Focus on native and non-native accents from different parts of the world with scripted speech.
Focus on suprasegmental features of unscripted speech.
Improving productive phonological accommodation; dictogloss and shadowing.
The role of intonation and pitch on conveying attitudes or emotion.
Focus on rhythm; mora-timed rhythm (Japanese) vs stress-timed rhythm (English)
First student project: Transcription and Presentation Project

textbooks: The teacher will provide all materials.
prereq: Students must be self motivated and willing to use English at all times during the seminar.
prep: Students will be required to review work done in class and read to prepare for future classes. Projects and assignments will also require work outside of class.
teachers: ◎ ショーン バーゴイン
